summary: Matched a large slice of sysdolphin baselib synth code, adding concrete implementations and prototypes for HSD audio/SFX loading, bank management, pause/resume/check, pitch/volume/mix update helpers, and the main HSD_SynthCallback. The PR also refined synth static data layout, named several synth BSS/SBSS symbols, split data string labels in symbols.txt, and tightened Dolphin AX/DevCom/debug header types needed by the matches. decomp.dev reported +3204 bytes of matched code, 21 new 100% matches, and partial progress on HSD_SynthSFXUpdateAllVolume and HSD_Synth_80388D30.

key_files.role: Main matching work; replaced many placeholder functions with C implementations for HSD_AudioMalloc/HSD_AudioFree, SFX load/cancel/bank helpers, pause/resume/check, volume/pitch/mix update helpers, and HSD_SynthCallback.
Added static globals, HSD_SynthSFXNode layout, SFX bank arrays, queue structs, callback pointers, and typed BSS/SBSS declarations required by synth.c.
Replaced many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with typed signatures and added AX include plus HSD_SynthSFXNode forward declaration.
Renamed/split synth data symbols, identified BSS regions such as hsd_SynthSFXBankHead, HSD_SynthSFXNodes, hsd_SynthSFXBankNum, hsd_SynthSFXBankAREnd, and sfxGroupDataReaddressCounter.
Corrected AXVPB linkage fields to struct _AXVPB* and changed priority from u32 to int.
Added HSD_ASSERTREPORT macro that emits OSReport before asserting.
Typed HSD_DevComCancelEx as int HSD_DevComCancelEx(int, int, void*, int).

what_changed: Implemented HSD_AudioMalloc using OSAllocFromHeap on HSD_Synth_804D6018, with an OSReport("audio heap overflow.\n") and __assert on failure, plus HSD_AudioFree using OSFreeToHeap.

Implemented SFX load queue management: HSD_SynthSFXLoadNewProc, HSD_SynthSFXLoad, HSD_Synth_80388B0C, HSD_Synth_80388B50, and HSD_Synth_80388B60. The code uses OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts around shared queue state and calls HSD_DevComRequest/HSD_DevComCancelEx.

Added bank/address helpers including HSD_Synth_80388CB0, partial HSD_Synth_80388D30, HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddressCallback, HSD_SynthSFXBankDeflag, HSD_SynthSFXBankDeflagSync, and HSD_SynthSFXStopRange.

Implemented sound mode and node lifecycle helpers: HSD_Synth_80389094 returns OSGetSoundMode, HSD_Synth_803890B4 updates active node mix and calls OSSetSoundMode, and HSD_Synth_8038912C frees voices and calls driverInactivatedCallback when applicable.
Added inline helper patterns in synth.c, including stopRange for address-range voice stopping and getNode for validating an SFX handle against HSD_SynthSFXNodes[arg0 & 0x3F].

Implemented public SFX control functions HSD_SynthSFXPause, HSD_SynthSFXResume, HSD_SynthSFXCheck, HSD_SynthSFXSetVolumeFade, HSD_SynthSFXSetMix, HSD_SynthSFXUpdatePitch, HSD_SynthSFXUpdateVolume, and partial HSD_SynthSFXUpdateAllVolume.
Implemented HSD_SynthCallback, including deferred AXFreeVoice processing, an 8-node-per-callback sweep based on HSD_Synth_804D775C % 8 * 8, master clock callback invocation, and interrupt protection.

Added concrete synth static data declarations, including HSD_SynthSFXNode, HSD_SynthSFXNodes[0x40], SFX load queue entries, bank head arrays, per-user volume data, callback pointers, volatile queue/readdress counters, and audio heap/devcom state.
Updated synth.h include guards from GALE01-style names to SYSDOLPHIN_SYNTH_H and converted many previously unknown prototypes to concrete return/argument types.
Split broad synth data symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt into individual string labels and corrected BSS/SBSS symbol boundaries for synth bank/node storage.
smart_moves: Header and static-layout work was done alongside function matching instead of only writing function bodies; typed AXVPB links, DevCom cancellation, callback pointers, and HSD_SynthSFXNode fields made downstream synth code expressible in C.
The PR used small static inline helpers, especially getNode and stopRange, to capture repeated compiler-shaped logic while keeping exported functions clean.
The HSD_ASSERTREPORT macro captured the common OSReport-then-assert pattern, avoiding local ad hoc code and allowing HSD_SynthSFXLoad and HSD_Synth_80388CB0 to match assertion/report behavior.
Renaming/splitting data symbols prevented broad anonymous blobs from hiding string boundaries and allowed more accurate references from matched code.
Shared audio queue and volume-update state was guarded with OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts, matching the expected critical-section patterns for callback-driven audio code.
AXVPB::priority was changed to signed int, which supports loops such as for (i = 0; i < temp_r29->priority; i++) without unsigned-comparison artifacts.
decomp_lessons: For baselib audio code, matching often depends on reconstructing static BSS/SBSS layout first; arrays like HSD_SynthSFXNodes, hsd_SynthSFXBankHead, HSD_Synth_804C2AE0, and HSD_Synth_804C2B60 clarified many pointer and index expressions.
Do not leave SDK structs as void* when linked-list traversal is visible. AXVPB next/prev needed to be struct _AXVPB* for natural list manipulation in bank defrag/free routines.
When an unknown global counter is polled or decremented asynchronously, volatile may be required; this PR marks HSD_Synth_804D772C and sfxGroupDataReaddressCounter volatile.
Callback signatures should be derived from call sites and SDK/devcom usage. The PR typed load/readdress callbacks with arguments like void*, int/s32, void*, and cancelflag.
SFX handles can encode a node slot in low bits; getNode validates arg0 > 0 and compares against node->x0 after indexing HSD_SynthSFXNodes[arg0 & 0x3F].
Volume and pitch update functions may queue work rather than directly mutate AX voice state. HSD_SynthSFXUpdateVolume links nodes through HSD_Synth_804D774C with a volume_update_pending flag.
Symbols.txt data blobs should be split once evidence shows string boundaries; this can unlock correct local label references without pretending unnamed strings have stable semantic names.
Partial matches can still be valuable if the surrounding type work is correct: decomp.dev reported HSD_SynthSFXUpdateAllVolume at 77.61% and HSD_Synth_80388D30 at 71.05% rather than full matches.
naming_conventions: Use HSD_SynthSFX* for named synth SFX APIs and helpers when behavior is known, e.g. HSD_SynthSFXLoad, HSD_SynthSFXPause, HSD_SynthSFXUpdatePitch, HSD_SynthSFXBankDeflag.
Keep address-style names such as HSD_Synth_80388B60 and HSD_Synth_804D772C when semantics are still unclear or not renamed in the PR.

Use lower camel-case for some recovered global variables that appear to have original-like names: hsd_SynthSFXBankHead, hsd_SynthSFXBankNum, hsd_SynthSFXBankAREnd, sfxGroupDataReaddressCounter, driverInactivatedCallback, driverMasterClockCallback, driverPauseCallback.
Use struct HSD_SynthSFXNode for the reconstructed node type and field placeholders like x0, x14, x2C, x44 where member semantics are only partially understood.
For config symbols, prefer lbl_8040xxxx for split local data/string labels when no semantic symbol name is known, rather than preserving one broad HSD_Synth_8040xxxx blob.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK in functions where stack shape mattered, including HSD_Synth_8038912C, HSD_SynthSFXUpdateAllVolume, and HSD_SynthCallback.
Used static inline helpers to reproduce codegen while avoiding extra exported symbols: stopRange, getNode, and freeVoices.
Used bool enabled = OSDisableInterrupts(); followed by OSRestoreInterrupts(enabled); to match critical-section codegen around shared synth state.
Preserved float constants as expressions like 1 / 255.0F * arg0 and 1 / 255.0 * arg1 where the literal form mattered for matching.
Used ARRAY_SIZE(HSD_SynthSFXNodes) in HSD_Synth_803890B4 while other loops explicitly use 0x40, reflecting local codegen needs.
Used pointer casts for hardware/AX address interpretation, e.g. reading a u32 through &voice[0]->pb.addr.currentAddressHi in stopRange.
Kept certain functions partially unmatched rather than forcing suspicious code: HSD_SynthSFXUpdateAllVolume and HSD_Synth_80388D30 were reported as improved but not 100%.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 matched code increased to 37.21%, +0.08%, +3204 bytes.

Reported 21 new 100% matches in main/sysdolphin/baselib/synth, including HSD_SynthCallback, HSD_SynthSFXLoad, HSD_Synth_80388B60, HSD_SynthSFXSetVolumeFade, HSD_Synth_8038912C, HSD_SynthSFXStopRange, HSD_SynthSFXUpdatePitch, HSD_SynthSFXBankDeflag, HSD_SynthSFXCheck, HSD_AudioMalloc, HSD_AudioFree, and HSD_Synth_80389094.
Reported 2 improvements in unmatched functions: HSD_SynthSFXUpdateAllVolume reached 77.61% and HSD_Synth_80388D30 reached 71.05%.
No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match HSD_SynthSFXUpdateAllVolume after this 77.61% partial implementation.
Find later PRs that finish HSD_Synth_80388D30 or rename HSD_Synth_80388DC8/HSD_Synth_80388E08.
Search for additional uses or refinements of struct HSD_SynthSFXNode fields x0, flags, voice_count, x2C, x44, x48, and x4C.
Search for other baselib modules that reuse HSD_ASSERTREPORT or need OSReport-before-assert matching.
Check whether AXVPB callback/userContext fields are further typed in later Dolphin AX header updates.
